$draft: DRAFT / SIMULATION-ONLY. NOT an immutable economy edition. Not consumed by any runtime, migration, disclosure, or the economy-simulator CLI. Mirrors the shape of economy/production/editions/0001-earned-collection.json acquisition.banner so the pity/rate-up numbers can be driven through the repo's exported pity engine (see simulate-premium-pity.mjs). Catalog ids prefixed 'draft-premium/' are PLACEHOLDERS for a to-be-produced rotating featured/collab die; a real edition MUST reference real src/generated/collectibleCatalog.json ids and go through /adr + a real migration.

currency.note: 1 premium_roll = 160 Stars-equivalent; premium banner consumes premium_roll, not standard_roll.

tiers.note: 0.60% base rate-up on the single featured die. Sole signature-tier item, so every signature hit is the featured die.

softPity.note: DESIGN TARGET, NOT CURRENT ENGINE. PO-accepted engine change (2026-07-22): add a soft-pity ramp to dicesuki-core / the 0011 pull policy + contracts. The engine TODAY still has none — the sim models this ramp externally via an injected draw wrapper (see simulate-premium-pity.mjs). Model: featured rate holds at base 0.006 through pull 40, then rises min(1, 0.006 + 0.005*(n-40)) from pull 41. Recommended +0.5%/pull slope (perPullIncrement 0.005): p50 ~51 pulls, ~2.2% still reach the hard-75 ceiling, mean ~46.6 pulls. Slope stays PO-tunable.

selectedFeaturedUnowned.note: Premium hard pity 75 is LOCKED (PO, 2026-07-22). Soft pity is a PO-ACCEPTED engine change (dicesuki-core / 0011 pull policy) but the CURRENT engine still has none; the structured softPity object above is the design target, not runtime behavior. Under the recommended +0.5%/pull ramp from pull 41, p50 drops to ~51 pulls and only ~2.2% of players reach the hard-75 ceiling (vs ~55% under the old no-soft-pity design). Standard banners keep a shallow/generous pity; premium is the revenue dial.
